headline: Security hardening across auth, uploads, backups, and the audit log
summary: A broad security-hardening release. It closes fail-open and encoding-mismatch gaps in the sign-in, CSRF, session, single-use-code, upload, backup, and audit-log paths; refreshes the vendored cryptography and identity framework to its current release; and raises the minimum Node.js runtime to 24.18.0.

items.title: Google sign-in domain gate rejects malformed addresses
items.body: The allowed-domains gate for Google sign-in now validates the address and derives the domain from its final "@". A crafted address carrying more than one "@" (for example user@allowed.example@attacker.example) previously had its domain read from the wrong segment, so it could satisfy an allowed-domains restriction while its real mailbox was elsewhere. Such an address is now refused.

items.title: CSRF same-origin check canonicalizes both origins
items.body: The JSON/multipart cross-origin check now normalizes the browser Origin and the configured origin the same way (host case, default port, trailing dot, and internationalized labels) before comparing. A legitimate same-origin request can no longer be refused — nor a cross-site one accepted — because the two sides used different encodings of the same host.

items.title: Single-use email access codes are consumed atomically
items.body: Redeeming a one-time bundle access code now claims it with an atomic compare-and-set. Two simultaneous redemptions of the same code can no longer both succeed; the second is refused.

items.title: A stored file's content type is bound to its actual bytes
items.body: When an uploaded file's declared type is one the server renders inline (an image or PDF) but its magic bytes do not match, it is now stored as a generic download type instead of the spoofed inline type, so the serve-time preview cannot be steered by a false Content-Type regardless of the file extension. File downloads and previews also carry X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff.

items.title: Off-site backups exclude the plaintext upload scratch directory
items.body: The backup walk no longer copies the transient chunk-assembly scratch directory (pre-assembly upload chunks that are not yet application-encrypted) to the off-site backup bucket. Only encrypted stored content is backed up.

items.title: Encrypted audit-log export verifies the archive before returning rows
items.body: Exporting a decrypted audit-log archive now verifies the archive's post-quantum signature (pinned to this server's own key history) and recomputes its tamper chain before any row is returned. A rewritten or wrong-key archive is refused rather than exported as authentic.

items.title: Idempotency-Key replay cache is scoped to the authenticated principal
items.body: The Idempotency-Key replay cache slot is now bound to the requesting principal, so one client's key can no longer collide with another's — closing a cross-actor denial where a shared or guessed key blocked another principal from using it.

items.title: Session refresh enforces the configured timeout floors
items.body: Refreshing a session's activity now re-checks the configured idle and absolute timeout floors, so an expired session cannot be revived by continued activity, and an active one is not over-expired by the framework defaults.

items.title: Time-window gates fail closed on a malformed value
items.body: The brute-force lockout backoff, the pending two-factor deadline, and invite expiry now treat an absent or unparseable timestamp as expired/locked rather than letting a non-finite value silently disable the check. Disabling two-factor is now rate-limited like the other code-checking endpoints.

items.title: Admin IP blocklist canonicalizes IPv6 entries
items.body: An IPv6 address added to the admin block list is now canonicalized to the same form the runtime peer lookup produces, so a block written in an expanded or alternate compression form takes effect instead of silently never matching.

items.title: Reserved object keys are refused in settings and stored-record merges
items.body: Administrative settings dispatch and the database overflow-field merge now reject reserved prototype keys and resolve entries by own-property lookup, so a request-supplied key cannot re-parent an object or pass an inherited member off as a known setting.

items.title: Untrusted paths and email headers route through the hardened primitives
items.body: Restore-time upload paths are resolved through the shared safe-path primitive, and email address validation and subject composition through the shared header-safety primitive that rejects carriage-return, line-feed, and NUL — closing header-injection and traversal classes at their source. API-key scope selection now matches whole scopes rather than a substring.

items.title: Minimum Node.js runtime raised to 24.18.0
items.body: The runtime floor is now Node.js 24.18.0 (24.17.0 was a security release; 24.18.0 bundles SQLite 3.53.1 and a backup-path fix, and guarantees the OpenSSL 3.5 post-quantum signature surface).

items.title: Refreshed the vendored cryptography and identity framework
items.body: The vendored cryptography and identity framework was updated to its current release, picking up its security hardening for HTTP message signatures, mail authentication, path-scoped routing, single-use token concurrency, and recursion and header-injection defenses.

items.title: Folder ZIP downloads use a query parameter for the folder path
items.body: The in-bundle folder download link now carries the folder path as a query parameter. The router refuses an encoded path separator in the request path, so nested folders are addressed via the query string rather than an encoded path segment.

items.title: Run Node.js 24.18.0 or newer
items.body: Upgrade the runtime to Node.js 24.18.0+ before deploying this release. The Docker image already pins a matching runtime; only non-container deployments need to act.
